title: Aurora retrieval graded-relevance rubric
scale: 0-3 graded relevance, used directly as the gain grade for NDCG
unit_of_evaluation: one documentation chunk from corpus aurora-docs-v1

grades.label: fully_sufficient
grades.anchor: The chunk on its own contains the complete, correct answer to the query. A reader needs nothing else.

grades.label: mostly_sufficient
grades.anchor: The chunk answers the main question but omits a detail the query implies, or requires trivial inference.

grades.label: related_supporting
grades.anchor: The chunk is on-topic and adds useful supporting context, but does not by itself answer the query.

grades.label: irrelevant
grades.anchor: The chunk does not help answer the query. Not listed as gold; absence from the gold set implies grade 0.

annotator_instructions: Read the query as an end user of the Aurora API would ask it.
Grade each candidate chunk independently against the anchors above; do not grade relative to what the retriever returned.
A query must have at least one grade-3 chunk (the primary evidence). Secondary chunks get grade 1 or 2.
Record why the top evidence is sufficient in the case's why_sufficient note so the label is auditable.
Development and held-out cases must use disjoint queries; never move a case between splits.
relevant_threshold_note: Recall@k and MRR treat any chunk with grade >= relevant_threshold (1) as relevant. NDCG uses the exact grade as the gain.
